**Prerequisites**
[Sniping Duo Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3258)

</column>

---

You aim a shot at your spotter, intending for your spotter to redirect that shot off their own weapon or shield to hit your foe from a better angle, and avoid your foe's cover. If your next action is to make a ranged Strike, you treat your spotter's position as your own for the purposes of determining cover and range. Your spotter must be within your weapon's first range increment and they must use a reaction to redirect your shot.
name: Targeted Redirection

    right="Feat 8"

[Duo's Aim](/Feats.aspx?ID=3262) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
</traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Sniping Duo Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3258)

</column>

---

With assistance from your spotter, you aim for an especially accurate attack. Make a ranged weapon Strike against a foe either within your spotter's melee reach or the first range increment of a ranged weapon your spotter is wielding. On this Strike, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the attack roll and ignore the target's concealment. If you're using a firearm with the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=409) trait, you don't take the normal circumstance penalty to this Strike for not having the required Strength score or firing without using a tripod.
name: Duo's Aim

<title

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Vantage Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=3263) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 139

**Archetype** [Sniping Duo](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=121)

**Prerequisites**
[Sniping Duo Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3258)

</column>

---

You and your spotter take advantage of each other's attacks to momentarily hide and throw your foes off-balance against your follow-up attack. When you successfully make a ranged Strike against a foe, your spotter can use a reaction to attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check against the foe's Perception DC. On a success, the foe is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the spotter's next attack before the end of your spotter's next turn. Similarly, when your spotter successfully makes a ranged Strike against a foe, you can use a reaction to attempt a Stealth check against the foe's Perception DC. On a success, the foe is flat-footed against your next attack against them before the end of your next turn.
name: Vantage Shot

    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tag Team](/Feats.aspx?ID=3264) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 139

**Archetype** [Sniping Duo](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=121)

**Prerequisites**
[Sniping Duo Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3258)

**Trigger**
You or your spotter misses with a Strike against a creature, and the creature is within the other's melee reach or first range increment.

</column>

---

Your skilled teamwork with your spotter enables you both to assist one another when you falter, using either other's failures as opportunities to strike. If you used Tag Team after your spotter missed the triggering Strike, make a ranged Strike against the same target with a –2 penalty. If you used this reaction after you missed the triggering Strike, your spotter can use their reaction to make a melee or ranged Strike against the same target. Strikes granted by this feat don't count toward your or your spotter's multiple attack penalty and your or your spotter's multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to the granted Strike.
name: Tag Team

[Concentrated Assault](/Feats.aspx?ID=3266) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

</column>

---

You and your spotter combine your efforts to bring down your target. If you and your spotter both use the Ready activity to make a Strike against the same opponent and choose the same trigger for the readied action, resolve your attacks at the same time. Each of you can use the higher of the two attack rolls for your Strikes, each using your own modifier; this is a fortune effect. If both attacks are successful, total the damage for both attacks for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.
name: Concentrated Assault

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spellshot Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3267) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Class" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=397" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 140

**Archetype** [Spellshot](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=122)

**Prerequisites**
way of the spellshot

</column>

---

You cast arcane spells like a [wizard](/Classes.aspx?ID=39), gaining a [spellbook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2753) with four common [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) cantrips of your choice. You gain the Cast a Spell activity. You can prepare two cantrips each day from your spellbook. You’re trained in the spell attack modifier and spell DC statistics. Your key spellcasting attribute for spellshot archetype spells is Intelligence, and they are arcane spells. You become trained in Arcana; if you were already trained in Arcana, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. This counts as the wizard archetype for the benefits of [Basic Wizard Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5109).

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat other than [Beast Gunner Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3229) until you've gained two other feats from the spellshot or beast gunner archetypes.

<title level="2">Spellshot Dedication leads to...</title>
[Black Powder Embodiment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3271), [Call Gun](/Feats.aspx?ID=3269), [Fulminating Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=3268), [Phase Bullet](/Feats.aspx?ID=3270), [Spell-Woven Shot](/Feats.aspx?ID=7437)
name: Spellshot Dedication

At 12th level, this increases to 2d6 additional damage, and at 18th level, it increases to 3d6 additional damage.

**Archetypes** [Spellshot](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=122), [Beast Gunner](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=116) (Level 8), [Beast Gunner](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=363) (Level 8)

---

You build a powerful magical connection with a chosen gun or crossbow, gaining the ability to conjure it directly to your hand from any distant location. During your daily preparations, choose a single crossbow or firearm. Until your next daily preparations, you can use the Call Gun action to call the gun to your hand.

 **Call Gun** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Effect** You hold aloft a free hand and call the firearm or crossbow you chose during your daily preparations into your hand. As long as the weapon you chose is on the same plane, it appears in your hand.
name: Call Gun

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

You can aim your bullets not just in three normal dimensions, but in additional dimensions as well, removing all obstacles between you and your target. Attempt a crossbow or firearm [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against a foe who’s [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84) or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to you (but not [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96)). The ammunition travels to your target in a straight line, passing through any non‑magical barriers or walls in its way, though magical barriers and force effects stop the bullet. The shot ignores all [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372), the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition, the hidden condition, and circumstance bonuses to AC from shields. It has a +4 status bonus to hit creatures wearing any type of armor. The Strike’s damage can’t be reduced with a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) reaction using a non‑magical shield.
name: Phase Bullet

---

Who needs skill when you've got dumb luck? Sometimes your shots hit miraculously, regardless of the fact that you seemed to slip when you pulled the trigger or that you weren't aiming at your foe when you fired. You gain the Accidental Shot activity.

 **Accidental Shot** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You make a Strike with a ranged weapon, rolling the attack and damage twice and using the better results for each. The attack ignores circumstance penalties to the attack roll and any flat check required due to the target being concealed or hidden.

<title level="2">Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication leads to...</title>
[Chain Reaction](/Feats.aspx?ID=3279), [I Meant to Do That](/Feats.aspx?ID=3278), [Lucky Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=3273), [No Hard Feelings](/Feats.aspx?ID=3274), [That was a Close One, Huh?](/Feats.aspx?ID=3275), [Unbelievable Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=3277), [Unbelievable Luck](/Feats.aspx?ID=3276)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chain Reaction](/Feats.aspx?ID=3279) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 143

**Archetype** [Unexpected Sharpshooter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=123)

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3272)

</column>

---

You fire your gun once, creating a devastating and unpredictable chain of events; perhaps your bullet strikes exactly the right spot on a water tower, causing it to flood and incite a stampede of horses which knock over a lantern that sets a city on fire. Whatever the exact chain reaction, and no matter how improbable, the indiscriminate catastrophe creates significant challenges for your enemies across a wide area while leaving everyone else alone.

 Make a Strike with a ranged weapon, and if you hit, you can make another Strike at a target within 30 feet of the first target. If you hit the second target, attempt to Strike a third target within 30 feet of the second target, and so on, continuing until you miss a target. You can cease the chain at any point; otherwise, it ends when you first miss an attack. However, you can't target the same creature more than once; each time you move to a new target, it must be a target you haven't made a Strike against yet during this particular use of Chain Reaction.

 This damage is caused by some improbable set of events that injures one target after another, rather than from actually making several shots. Perhaps you shot at a tree branch that then fell on the targets, or one target's scream caused another to accidentally set off their gun and shoot themselves in the foot. This means that only the first target suffers any special effects tied to the bullet (if it was magical ammunition, for example), but each target you hit still takes the same damage dealt by your Strike. Normally, all damage is of the same damage type as the initial bullet. However, the GM might choose to change the damage type based on their description of the chain reaction. For example, if one target was burned by fire, that target might take fire damage instead of physical damage.
name: Chain Reaction

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 200

**Access**
You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

</column>

---

You're used to the clamor of steel, the crack of a firearm, and the thunderous blast of explosions. You gain resistance 3 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) and [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147). If you roll a success on a saving throw against an effect that causes the [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) conditions, you get a critical success instead.
name: Blast Resistance

    level="1"
    right="Feat 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spark Fist](/Feats.aspx?ID=3286) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 200

**Frequency**
once per minute

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

**Access**
You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

</column>

---

You dust explosive black powder on your fist or glove before attacking, which combusts as you hit an opponent. The resulting pops of flame harm both you and your enemy. Until the end of your next turn, your fist loses the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait and deals an additional 1 fire damage on a successful Strike. Each time you succeed on a fist Strike, you take 1 fire damage. If your fist would deal more than one weapon damage die, the fire damage dealt on a successful Strike, to both you and your opponent, is equal to the number of weapon damage dice.
name: Spark Fist

    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crafter's Instinct](/Feats.aspx?ID=3289) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 201

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

**Access**
You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

**Trigger**
You misfire with a firearm or you roll a failure on a Strike with a ranged weapon.

</column>

---

You sense a minor flaw in the weapon as you fire it, and quickly adjust your aim on the fly to avoid the flaw. If the triggering Strike was a [misfire](/Rules.aspx?ID=1649), you get a normal failure instead. If the triggering Strike was a failure, it becomes a glancing blow that deals minimum damage for the Strike (adding any bonuses as normal but getting a result of 1 for all damage dice that would be rolled on a success). The glancing blow does not apply other effects that would normally happen only on a hit.
name: Crafter's Instinct

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scrutinizing Gaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=3291) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 201

**Access**
You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

</column>

---

Your family's traditions of defending against Geb's [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) have granted you a sixth sense for detecting spirits, haunts, and other restless dead, no matter their form or the strength of their presence. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85) when trying to determine if a creature is possessed or under the influence of an effect that would make them [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks made to see through disguises worn by undead, and a +2 circumstance bonus when using the Seek action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=88) or undead within 30 feet of you.

 If you aren't using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action or searching, the GM automatically rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) check for you to notice haunts or undead within 30 feet anyway. This check doesn't gain the usual +2 circumstance bonus, and instead takes a –2 circumstance penalty.
name: Scrutinizing Gaze

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Harsh Judgment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3330) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Vigilante" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=326" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 64

**Archetype** [Gray Gardener](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=124)

**Prerequisites**
[Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); member of the Gray Gardeners

</column>

---

Choose one creature you can see, and loudly declare the creature's life forfeit by your judgment. That creature becomes your condemned foe until they are defeated, you use Harsh Judgment on a different creature, or the encounter ends. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) your condemned foe and on Intimidation checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) them. You have a –1 circumstance penalty to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) creatures other than your condemned foe and on Intimidation checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) anyone other than your condemned foe. You typically can use Harsh Judgment only during an encounter, and if you try to use it to declare someone's life forfeit during exploration or downtime, you are likely to start an encounter.

<title level="2">Harsh Judgment leads to...</title>
[Accurate Swing](/Feats.aspx?ID=3334), [Execution](/Feats.aspx?ID=3338), [Frightful Condemnation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3333)
name: Harsh Judgment
prerequisite_markdown: [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); member of the Gray Gardeners

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Vigilante" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=326" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 65

**Archetype** [Gray Gardener](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=124)

**Prerequisites**
[Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); [Harsh Judgment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3330); member of the Gray Gardeners

</column>

---

You strike cleanly with wide, sweeping attacks to inflict maximum damage, taking advantage of your judged foe's fear to land a felling blow. If you Strike a creature you condemned with Harsh Judgment that has the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition with a [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194) melee weapon or a [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194) unarmed attack, you gain a circumstance bonus to the Strike's damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice your weapon deals.
name: Accurate Swing
prerequisite_markdown: [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); [Harsh Judgment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3330); member of the Gray Gardeners

</title>

once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); member of the Gray Gardeners

**Trigger**
You roll a failure (but not a critical failure) on a Will save against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effect

</column>

---

The Gray Gardeners are known for having nerves of steel and an unbreakable resolve. When you would be overwhelmed by terror, you can draw upon this resolve to keep your composure and remain undaunted. You take mental damage equal to twice your level, and the saving throw is a success.
name: Gardener's Resolve
prerequisite_markdown: [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); member of the Gray Gardeners

    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Strident Command](/Feats.aspx?ID=3337) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 65

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7); [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); [Voice of Authority](/Feats.aspx?ID=3331); member of the Gray Gardeners

</column>

---

The common folk heed your demands, and though your true foes rarely bend to your commands, when they do the hesitation can cost them the battle. You can cast [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) once every 10 minutes as a 1st-level occult innate spell. 

If you're in your vigilante identity, you can command the target only to approach you, drop prone, or stand in place. If you're in your social identity, you can command the target only to run away or release what the target is holding.
name: Strident Command
prerequisite_markdown: Master in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7); [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); [Voice of Authority](/Feats.aspx?ID=3331); member of the Gray Gardeners

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Execution](/Feats.aspx?ID=3338) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Vigilante" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=326" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Night of the Gray Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=99) pg. 65

**Prerequisites**
[Harsh Judgment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3330); [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); member of the Gray Gardeners

**Requirements**
You have condemned a foe with [Harsh Judgment.](/Feats.aspx?ID=3330)

</column>

---

You swing wide, attempting to behead your condemned foe. Make a melee Strike against your condemned foe. If you hit, your condemned foe takes 8d6 extra precision damage with a basic Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. If the condemned foe is reduced to 0 Hit Points by the execution and requires a head to live, they die. For creatures with multiple heads, this usually kills the creature only if you sever its last head. The creature then becomes temporarily immune to your Execution for 1 day.
name: Execution
prerequisite_markdown: [Harsh Judgment](/Feats.aspx?ID=3330); [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083); member of the Gray Gardeners
requirement_markdown: You have condemned a foe with [Harsh Judgment.](/Feats.aspx?ID=3330)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Harmless Doll](/Feats.aspx?ID=3340) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Poppet" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=418" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 63

</column>

---

You look like nothing more than an ordinary toy, doll, or statuette, and you can fool others with your innocuous appearance. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) (or another skill if you're already trained in Deception), and you can [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) an inanimate toy or a mindless poppet. You can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) without any [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) or [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from creatures that don't realize you're alive, so long as you're in a location where a toy of your shape wouldn't be out of place, like a carnival booth, city street, or toy shop (at the GM's discretion). If you succeed, onlookers still see you, but they mistake you for an inanimate toy. After being fooled once, they realize you're alive, and you can't Hide from them in that way again.
name: Harmless Doll

<traits>
<trait label="Poppet" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=418" />

<column gap="tiny">

Time spent tending to your own form has unlocked your ability to fix other things. You can cast 1st-level [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) once per day as an arcane innate spell. At 7th level, the spell is heightened to 2nd level, and every 3 levels thereafter, the spell is heightened an additional spell level. Even though you're alive and not an object, and you can't normally recover Hit Points from effects that repair objects (like the [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) skill), you can target yourself with this innate spell and use it to recover Hit Points.
name: Cunning Tinker

[Impossible Gossip](/Feats.aspx?ID=3353) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Poppet" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=418" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 65

</column>

---

You can speak to inanimate objects, finding a magical spark within them similar to the one that animated you. You can cast [_stone tell_](/Spells.aspx?ID=310) once per day as an arcane innate spell, but instead of speaking with natural or worked stone, you speak with toys, statues, or other three-dimensional inanimate objects crafted to mimic a creature's appearance, regardless of their composition. The GM has discretion over which objects you can speak with and what they know, although objects generally have a good opinion of those who have cared for them and a poor opinion of those who have neglected or ignored them.
name: Impossible Gossip

You gain or increase the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=42) condition as normal when losing the dying condition in this way.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Captivator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3358) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 120

**Archetype** [Captivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=125)

**Prerequisites**
Charisma 14; Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6)

</column>

---

Your ability to captivate others borders on the preternatural. You are seldom at a loss in social situations, and you can create minor [enchantments](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) or [illusions](/Traits.aspx?ID=92). Choose Deception or Diplomacy. You become trained in that skill, or become an expert if you were already trained.

 Choose two cantrips from the [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) list; each cantrip must be from either the enchantment or illusion school. You gain access to the Cast a Spell activity and can cast these spells as occult innate spells. You're trained in occult spell attack rolls and spell DCs. If you have a hand free, you can usually replace material components with somatic components, so you don't need to use a spell [component pouch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=33). Your key spellcasting ability for these spells is Charisma.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the captivator archetype.

<title level="2">Captivator Dedication leads to...</title>
[Basic Captivator Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=3359), [Effortless Captivation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3365), [Heightened Captivation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3362), [Look Again](/Feats.aspx?ID=3367), [Reactive Charm](/Feats.aspx?ID=3364)
name: Captivator Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: Charisma 14; Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Basic Captivator Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=3359) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 120

**Archetype** [Captivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=125)

**Prerequisites**
[Captivator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3358)

</column>

---

Your innate ability to fascinate others develops into full-fledged spellcasting. This spellcasting comes naturally and instinctively to you, rather than as a product of training. Choose a 1st-level occult spell from either the [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) or [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) school. You can Cast this Spell as an occult innate spell. At 6th level, you gain a 2nd-level spell, and at 8th level, you gain a 3rd-level spell. Each of these spells must be from either the enchantment or illusion school.

<title level="2">Basic Captivator Spellcasting leads to...</title>
[Captivating Intensity](/Feats.aspx?ID=3360), [Countercharm](/Feats.aspx?ID=3361), [Expert Captivator Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=3363)
name: Basic Captivator Spellcasting

<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Captivating Intensity](/Feats.aspx?ID=3360) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 121

**Archetype** [Captivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=125)

**Prerequisites**
[Basic Captivator Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=3359)

</column>

---

Your ability to captivate others has grown, and you can use your captivator abilities more often. You seem to never run out of the ability to fascinate or distract. You can cast each occult innate spell granted by captivator archetype feats one additional time per day. You can do this for spells of all levels granted, other than your two highest spell levels from non-cantrip captivator spells. If you have [Heightened Captivation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3362), you can Cast the chosen Spell a second time each day, though one of the two times you cast it, it's heightened two levels lower than your highest level of non-cantrip captivator spells, as long as the spell can be cast at that spell level.
name: Captivating Intensity

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Countercharm](/Feats.aspx?ID=3361) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 121

**Archetype** [Captivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=125)

**Prerequisites**
[Basic Captivator Spellcasting](/Feats.aspx?ID=3359)

**Trigger**
A creature Casts a Spell from the [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) or [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) school.

**Requirements**
You have an innate non-cantrip captivator spell of the same school.

</column>

---

Your enchantments and illusions are so dominant that you can use them to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) similar magic cast by others. When a foe casts an enchantment or illusion spell and you can see its manifestations, you can use your own innate magic to disrupt it. You lose an innate, non-cantrip spell of the same school as the triggering spell as if you had Cast the Spell. You then attempt to counteract the triggering spell with your spell.
name: Countercharm
requirement_markdown: You have an innate non-cantrip captivator spell of the same school.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Heightened Captivation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3362) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Metamagic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=107" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 121

**Archetype** [Captivator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=125)

**Prerequisites**
[Captivator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=3358)

</column>

---

You can infuse your [enchantments](/Traits.aspx?ID=61) and [illusions](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) with more power. Choose one of your spells granted by captivator archetype feats; this spell can't be from your highest level of non-cantrip captivator spells. The spell you choose is heightened to the highest level of captivator spell you can cast, not including cantrips. You can't later change the spell that is heightened by this feat.

**Special** You can select this feat more than once. Each time you select it, you choose a different spell.
name: Heightened Captivation
